name: INVO Fertility, Inc.
business: INVO Fertility is a healthcare services and technology company focused on the fertility marketplace that acquires, establishes, and operates fertility clinics offering IVF and the IVC procedure enabled by its INVOcell device; it also sells and distributes the INVOcell device to third-party clinics.

exposures.sentence: Our proprietary INVOcell device enables fertilization and early embryo development to occur in vivo within the woman s body - the world s first intravaginal culture (IVC) technique of its kind.
Our acquisition strategy focuses on US-based, profitable fertility clinics.

catalysts.event: Completed acquisition of Family Beginnings fertility clinic
catalysts.sentence: On February 18, 2026, we completed our acquisition of Family Beginnings P.C., a fertility clinic located in Indianapolis, Indiana.

catalysts.event: Entered into binding term sheet to settle Pritts litigation
catalysts.sentence: On May 14, 2025, INVO, Dr. Pritts, the Pritts Trust, and certain of their respective affiliates entered into binding term sheet (the Term Sheet) to settle all disputes between the parties pursuant to the terms set forth in the Term Sheet (the Terms).

catalysts.event: Received FDA 510(k) clearance for 5-day incubation labeling expansion
catalysts.sentence: In June 2023, we received FDA 510(k) clearance to expand the labeling on the INVOcell device and its indication for use to provide for a 5-day incubation period.

catalysts.event: Warrant inducement transaction raising $7.5 million gross proceeds
catalysts.sentence: On January 28, 2026, we entered into an inducement letter agreement (the January 2026 Inducement Letter Agreement) with an institutional investor and existing holder (the Holder) of the Common Warrants... The aggregate gross proceeds to us from the exercise of such existing warrants was approximately $7.5 million.

tamClaims.claim: One in six couples worldwide experience fertility challenges
tamClaims.sentence: According to the European Society for Human Reproduction s 2024 ART Fact Sheet, one in six couples worldwide experience fertility challenges.

tamClaims.claim: Less than 5% of infertile couples worldwide receive treatment and less than 2% have a child through IVF
tamClaims.sentence: Regrettably, this only amounts to less than 5% of the infertile couples worldwide being treated and less than 2% of such couples having a child though IVF.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 435,000 IVF cycles performed in the U.S. across about 500 IVF centers
tamClaims.sentence: Based on 2022 data from the CDC s National ART Surveillance System, approximately 435,000 IVF cycles were performed across ~500 IVF centers, leaving the U.S. with a large, underserved patient population.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 4 million ART cycles performed globally each year, producing around 1 million babies
tamClaims.sentence: There have been large increases in the use of IVF, with current estimates of approximately 4 million ART cycles performed globally each year, producing around 1 million babies.
